title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Mississippi
description: This race is for Mississippi’s Class II seat in the U.S. Senate, currently held by Republican Cindy Hyde-Smith. It matters because the seat is part of the Senate’s 2026 balance of power, but Mississippi remains a strongly Republican state, so the general election is likely to hinge more on candidate contrast, turnout, and fundraising than on partisan competitiveness. The field includes an incumbent Republican, a Democratic district attorney, and independent and minor-party challengers, with attention centered on healthcare, costs, jobs, public safety, and ethics.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race after 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Cindy Hyde-Smith
candidates.summary: Cindy Hyde-Smith is the incumbent U.S. senator from Mississippi and won the Republican nomination in the 2026 primary. She previously served as Mississippi commissioner of agriculture and commerce and in the state legislature, and her campaign continues to emphasize conservative priorities and her alignment with Republican leadership.

summary_sources.title: Hyde-Smith wins GOP nomination, Colom secures Democratic nod ...

career_history.description: Represents Mississippi in the U.S. Senate.

career_history.title: Commissioner of Agriculture and Commerce
career_history.description: Served as Mississippi’s agriculture commissioner before joining the Senate.

candidates.name: Scott Colom
candidates.summary: Scott Colom is the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in Mississippi and serves as district attorney for the 16th Judicial District. His campaign has focused on lowering costs, protecting hospitals, and expanding economic opportunity, while highlighting his record as a prosecutor and his earlier federal judicial nomination.

summary_sources.title: Hyde-Smith wins GOP nomination, Colom secures Democratic nod ...
Scott Colom for Senate – Mississippi Matters

candidates.name: Ty Pinkins
candidates.summary: Ty Pinkins is running for U.S. Senate in Mississippi as an independent after previously being the Democratic nominee for the seat in 2024. He is a U.S. Army veteran and attorney whose campaign has centered on housing, education, healthcare, and jobs, and he has framed his bid as a challenge to the state’s two-party system.

summary_sources.title: Ty Pinkins rocks political boat for 2026 US Senate bid - WJTV
Ty Pinkins for US Senate | Mississippi

candidates.name: Sarah Adlakha
candidates.summary: Sarah Adlakha ran in the Republican primary for U.S. Senate in Mississippi and is a physician by profession. Her campaign emphasized healthcare, affordability, education, border security, fiscal restraint, and ethics reform, but she lost the primary to incumbent Cindy Hyde-Smith.

candidates.name: Albert R. Littell
candidates.summary: Albert R. Littell was a Democratic candidate in Mississippi’s 2026 U.S. Senate race and campaigned on living wages and affordable healthcare. He was one of the Democratic primary candidates but did not emerge as the nominee.

summary_sources.title: Albert Littell Vows $20 Living Wage if Elected to US Senate

candidates.name: Priscilla W. Till
candidates.summary: Priscilla W. Till was a Democratic candidate in Mississippi’s 2026 U.S. Senate race and ran on a platform focused on change and public service. She was one of the Democratic primary candidates but did not win the nomination.

summary_sources.title: Priscilla Williams Till: A choice for real change - Jackson Advocate
